county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1851 from Monroe, Washington, Morgan, Guernsey.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Caldwell. Created 1851 — Ohio's last county to be formed. Check Monroe, Washington, Morgan, and Guernsey for pre-1851 records. Rural Appalachian county with sparse population. Noble County Historical Society. Wolf Creek area. Oil and gas production records (1860s+). One of Ohio's least populous counties. Strong Baptist and Methodist church records. Congress Lands survey system.

source_profiles.repository: Noble County Recorder, Caldwell
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1851. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Noble County Probate Court, Caldwell
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1851. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Noble County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.repository: Noble County Probate Court, Caldwell
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1851. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Port Clinton. Lake Erie islands (Put-in-Bay, Kelleys Island). Battle of Lake Erie (1813) — War of 1812 records. Perry's Victory and International Peace Memorial. Created 1840 from Erie, Sandusky, and Lucas. Ottawa County Historical Society. Resort and tourism area — hotel and tourism employment records. Commercial fishing records on Lake Erie. German immigrant community. Check parent counties for pre-1840 records.

source_profiles.repository: Ottawa County Recorder, Port Clinton
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1840. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Ottawa County Probate Court, Port Clinton
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1840. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Ottawa County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.repository: Ottawa County Probate Court, Port Clinton
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1840. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.repository: Ottawa County Clerk of Courts, Port Clinton / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. Ohio had massive European immigration — German, Irish, Welsh, English, Italian, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, Czech, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Ohio naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

county_metadata.record_losses: 1871 courthouse fire destroyed some early records including deeds and court records
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Paulding. BURNED COUNTY — 1871 courthouse fire destroyed some early deed and court records. Created 1820, organized 1839. Located in the Black Swamp region — late settlement. Paulding County Carnegie Library has local history collection. Flat Rock Creek and Auglaize River area. Reservoir War (1887) over canal reservoir demolition. Extensive drainage improvement records document land transformation. Very late settlement compared to eastern Ohio. Check Darke County for early records.

source_profiles.repository: Paulding County Recorder, Paulding
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1871 fire destroyed some early deed books; surviving records may have gaps

source_profiles.repository: Paulding County Probate Court, Paulding
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1820. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some early records lost in 1871 fire; later records intact

source_profiles.source_name: Paulding County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.repository: Paulding County Probate Court, Paulding
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1820. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some early probate records affected by 1871 fire

source_profiles.source_name: Paulding County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Paulding County Clerk of Courts, Paulding
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1820. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in New Lexington. Created 1817 from Washington, Muskingum, and Fairfield. Named for Commodore Oliver Hazard Perry. Coal mining county — mine records document immigrant workers. Perry County Historical Society. New Lexington and Shawnee area. Sunday Creek valley coal fields. Significant pottery industry records. Congress Lands survey system. Strong German and Irish immigrant communities in mining areas.

source_profiles.repository: Perry County Recorder, New Lexington
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1817. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1817. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1817. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Circleville. Created 1810 from Ross, Fairfield, and Franklin. Located in the Virginia Military District — VA bounty land warrants. Circleville is named for the ancient circular earthwork (now destroyed). Ted Lewis birthplace. Pickaway County Historical Society. Circleville Pumpkin Show (1903+) is a community event with early newspaper coverage. Scioto River valley agricultural records. Strong early German and Scots-Irish settlement.

source_profiles.repository: Pickaway County Recorder, Circleville
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1810. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1810. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Military District Land Warrants — Pickaway County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Library of Virginia / BLM-GLO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Virginia military bounty land warrants issued to Virginia Revolutionary War veterans. The Virginia Military District covered south-central Ohio between the Scioto and Little Miami Rivers. Warrants often bought and sold — chain of ownership can trace families. BLM-GLO has original federal patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records survive at multiple repositories — Ohio History Connection, Library of Virginia, and NARA

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Waverly. Created 1815 from Ross, Scioto, and Adams. Located in the Virginia Military District. Pike County Historical Society. Scioto River and Scioto Brush Creek valleys. Pike Lake State Park area. Camp Charlotte (1774 Dunmore's War) historical site. Check parent counties for pre-1815 records. Rural Appalachian county — church records are essential supplements. Waverly named for Sir Walter Scott's novels.

source_profiles.repository: Pike County Recorder, Waverly
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1815. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Ravenna. Western Reserve county. Created 1807 from Trumbull. Kent State University archives. Portage County Historical Society in Ravenna. Portage Lakes area. Connecticut Land Company records at WRHS, Cleveland. Hiram College (1850, Disciples of Christ) archives. James A. Garfield attended Hiram College. Strong New England heritage. Well-preserved early court and deed records from 1807.

source_profiles.repository: Portage County Recorder, Ravenna
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1807. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.

source_profiles.source_name: Connecticut Western Reserve Land Records — Portage County
source_profiles.repository: Western Reserve Historical Society, Cleveland / CT State Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Connecticut ceded its Western Reserve to the federal government in 1800, but settlers arrived from 1796. Connecticut Land Company records document original purchasers and settlers. Check Connecticut state records for settler origins.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at WRHS in Cleveland; Connecticut State Library also has copies

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Eaton. Created 1808 from Montgomery and Butler. Located partly in Congress Lands and partly in Symmes Purchase. Preble County Historical Society in Eaton. Fort St. Clair (1792) site — Indian War era records. Strong Quaker settlement — Camden and West Elkton Friends meeting records. Twin Creek and Four Mile Creek valleys. Agricultural county near Indiana border. Check Montgomery and Butler for pre-1808 records.

source_profiles.repository: Preble County Recorder, Eaton
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1808. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1808. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).

source_profiles.source_name: Symmes Purchase Land Records — Preble County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Cincinnati History Library and Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: John Cleves Symmes purchased approximately 1 million acres between the Great Miami and Little Miami Rivers. Records document original patent holders and early settlers in SW Ohio.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records at Ohio History Connection and Cincinnati History Library and Archives

source_profiles.source_name: Society of Friends (Quaker) Meeting Records — Preble County
source_profiles.repository: Wilmington College Quaker Heritage Center / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Monthly meeting records include births, marriages, deaths, removals (transfers), and disownments. Quakers kept meticulous records. Ohio Yearly Meeting and Indiana Yearly Meeting records at Wilmington College.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptionally well-preserved; many microfilmed and indexed

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Ottawa (village). Created 1820, organized 1834. Very strong German Catholic heritage — one of the most Catholic counties in the United States. Kalida, Columbus Grove, Leipsic, and Continental church records essential. Putnam County Historical Society. Miami & Erie Canal corridor. Check Shelby County for early records. Ottawa is named differently from Ottawa County (which has Port Clinton as seat). Blanchard River area.

source_profiles.repository: Putnam County Recorder, Ottawa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1820. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1820. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection, Columbus
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. The Ohio & Erie Canal (Cleveland to Portsmouth) and Miami & Erie Canal (Cincinnati to Toledo) transformed Ohio's economy and brought many immigrants.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.source_name: German Catholic Parish Records — Putnam County
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archdiocese of Cincinnati / Parish offices
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Baptism, marriage, and burial records from German Catholic parishes. Many parishes kept records in Latin or German. Parish registers often include parents' birthplaces in Germany. Archdiocese of Cincinnati central archives hold some consolidated records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Parish records generally well-preserved; some microfilmed by FamilySearch

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Mansfield. Created 1808 from Fairfield. Located in Congress Lands. Mansfield is a major city — Mansfield/Richland County Public Library has genealogy collection. Ohio State Reformatory (Mansfield, 1896) prison records. Richland County Historical Society. Johnny Appleseed territory. Malabar Farm (Louis Bromfield) area. Blockhouse site from War of 1812 era. Strong German and Scots-Irish heritage. Check Fairfield County for pre-1808 records.

source_profiles.repository: Richland County Recorder, Mansfield
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1808. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / NARA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ohio Roster Commission published 'Official Roster of the Soldiers of the State of Ohio in the War of the Rebellion' (12 volumes)
